entry.title: Manejo de descuentos en nóminas anticipadas cuando hay bajas
entry.content:
Como se debería resolver esto que se requiere: cuando se detecte que un colaborador cause baja y ya se encuentre con este estatus en ECP, los siguientes conceptos sean bloqueados tanto de su ultima nomina ordinaria como del calculo de finiquito. 

 

2760 CAJA AHORR EMPL. 

2762 CAJA AHORRO A PLAZOS 

2745 DSC PREST CJA AHORRO  

 

Ejemplo: 

 

En SPF, la nomina semanal se paga a perido vencido, por lo que aunque aun le corresponda el pago de una ultima nomina si detecta que el colaborador ya es baja al momento del calculo, ya no reconozca la deducción de los conceptos antes mencionados, y adicional, cuando se calcule el finiquito, en caso de tener alguno de ellos capturados en el periodo de este, no los reconozca.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the New PaPM Step in Multi Actions in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@SarauShen - does this support PaPM Cloud Universal Model as well?

What data services api should we be using for PaPM Cloud Universal Model (LOD-PER-ACE)?

https://{host}.cfapps.{region}.hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/NXI/P1_PROCESSING

entry.title: Re: Any option to auto Approve/Reject a PO in My Inbox in...
entry.content: Hi @Gahan_Narayan1, in current version there is no API or BAdI can be used to auto approve a PO. As a workaround you may try to define a custom condition to start a PO workflow and set the action to auto approve https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/9a6d0c5e42d04d47aa40f51927c15453.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: What are the SAP HCM master data CDS views in S4 / Hana
entry.content:
Hello,

I am looking to have list of all SAP HCM CDS views in S4 especially e-Rec (a recruitment process) such as Requisition, Candidate Candidacy etc.

I will appreciate if any expert can list down the technical names of the CDS views here.

Regards

entry.title: Using overall goal rating only and leaving individual goal ratings blank
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Is there a way to be able to just use the overall goal rating instead of entering individual goals during a midyear PA review?

I know that setting manual-rating="true" can override the calculated value, but would doing that allow managers to leave the individual ratings blank?

 

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: Hard code bill to address for a PO
entry.content: Dear @Shama321, Would you please find BAdIs from the help document below? You may consider to add custom logic and set value for the billing to address for PO https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/8676e556cca85267e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Request for Standard SAP Functionality for managing c...
entry.content: Dear @Satish110, as advised by @Dennis_Suck85  afrom my point of view there is no standard app to manage freight and handling charges outside the PO (instead of inserting them while invoice receipt and working with statistical charges). I do not understand why you want to manage it in another screen, app? I cannot recommend to go to a custom development without having a clear picture what you aim to do?

entry.title: Re: SAP Desktop Assistant Learning Center - Default Searc...
entry.content:
Hi Christina,

There is an option to turn off the Book Page contents search option for standard libraries, I suspect that this will do the same thing for SAP Companion for Desktop Libraries.  To turn off search of Book Page contents, update the setting shown in the image below, it's located in the Playback Settings object in the Adaptable Resources in Producer.  Make sure that you synchronize the modified Playback Settings object to Manager and publish it.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: New SDK behavior with Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 (Professional)
entry.content: Hi Jayash, The debug issue is solved by installing new fix which has been released to customers via Software Center.

entry.title: Re: Add Object Page to Overview Page App in Fiori Elements?
entry.content: Hello Miguel,

entry.title: Re: Add Object Page to Overview Page App in Fiori Elements?

entry.title: Re: ADOBE Forms does not display custom field contents
entry.content: Hi Salim, I believe that you are using output odata service & form template with Brazil specific version, could you please have a try on normal version

entry.title: Re: Brazilian Localization: DDA - Débito Direto Automático. Any words?
entry.content:
Dear Daniel,

 Hope you remember me , we work for a brief period of time TCS-Roche 🙂 . And hope you are doing great,

 I am doing the implementation of this requirement. Users only is requesting to update the Boleto reference in the open invoice using the bank files. I can see based on this threads, we only need to implement the notes, make the relevant bank config for the segment G, and we should be ready to go to process through FF_5 and DDA_BR. We are still running in ECC 6.0  SAP_FIN 618. So most of the notes should be there. 

Just wanted to ask you before we start the project if there is any support document /configuration manual that you can help me to share that will help us to speed the process? 

I've noticed you shared something some long time back. 

If possible, it would be of great help: albor.arturo@gmail.com. 

Thank you , and happy to see you here.. as allways on top of everything.

I face an issue where the UI table did not show as selection. My CDS are Travel and Booking.

 

this is my metadata extension for Travel entity :

 

Not sure what mistake i made here while follow the sample.. 

Apprectiate if someone here can give feedback and help me go through while learning RAP.

Thanks.

entry.title: Beyond Approuter: Connecting an External Node.js App to SAP Cloud Identity Services with Standard OI
entry.content:
Introduction

For developers in the SAP BTP ecosystem, the standard path for authentication involves the SAP Application Router and XSUAA. It's robust and deeply integrated. But what if you need to connect a non-BTP application or simply want to experiment with open standards?

This guide demonstrates how to authenticate a standalone Node.js application directly against SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS) using a standard OpenID Connect (OIDC) flow.

Disclaimer: A Proof-of-Concept, Not a Production BTP Recipe

It's crucial to state this upfront: this approach is for educational and validation purposes. For production applications on SAP BTP, the Approuter/XSUAA model remains the recommended best practice. This method is perfect for learning, prototyping, or integrating external apps.

Part 1: Prerequisites - Setting up SAP CIS and Your Local Environment

Before we write any code, we need to configure our identity provider (CIS) and prepare our local machine.

Step 1: Subscribe to Cloud Identity Services

First, ensure you have a subscription to the Cloud Identity Services application in your BTP subaccount. You can add it via the "Service Marketplace" if you haven't already.

Step 2: Create an OIDC Application in SAP CIS

Navigate to your SAP CIS tenant's administration console. You can typically access this from the "Instances and Subscriptions" page in your BTP subaccount.

  1. In the CIS admin UI, go to Applications & Resources -> Applications.
  2. Click Create Application.
  3. Fill in the application details:
    • Name: localhost-auth-app (or any name you prefer)
    • Protocol: Select OpenID Connect from the dropdown.
       
  4. Add redirect URL.
    This will reveal the OIDC-specific configuration options. For now, we'll set placeholder values for the URIs.
    • Redirect URIs: https://localhost:3000/callback
    • Web Origins: https://localhost:3000
    • Click Save

Step 3: Collect Your Credentials and Issuer URL

Once the application is saved, CIS will generate the necessary credentials. Keep this page open or copy the following values to a safe place:

  • Client ID: A long, unique string.
  • Client Secret: Click the "show secret" icon to reveal and copy it.

    Next, you need the Issuer URL. This is the unique URL for your CIS tenant that OIDC clients use for discovery.

    1. In the CIS admin UI, navigate to Tenant Settings -> OpenID Connect Configuration.
    2. Under Identity Provider Settings, you will find the Issuer URL.

Copy this URL. It will look something like https://.trial-accounts.ondemand.com. For example: https://afsp7XXXX.trial-accounts.ondemand.com.

Step 4: Create a Self-Signed Certificate for Local HTTPS

Our Node.js application will run locally on https://localhost.

To enable HTTPS, which is required for OIDC flows, we need an SSL certificate. We can generate a self-signed one for development purposes using OpenSSL.

Open your terminal and run this command:

# This command creates a key.pem and cert.pem file valid for 365 days.
openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -sha256 -keyout key.pem -out cert.pem -days 365

You will be prompted to enter some information. You can press Enter to accept the defaults. This will generate key.pem and cert.pem in your current directory.

Part 2: Building the Node.js Application

Now we're ready to build the app.

Step 1: Project Setup

Create a new project folder and initialize it.

# Create project directory and navigate into it
mkdir cis-oidc-app && cd cis-oidc-app

# Initialize a Node.js project
npm init -y

# Install necessary libraries
npm install express openid-client@5.1.5 dotenv

# Create our application files
touch app.js .env

Note: We are pinning openid-client to a known stable version (5.1.5) to avoid potential issues with newer releases.

Step 2: Configure Environment Variables

Open the .env file and add the credentials you collected from CIS.

# .env file

# Paste the Issuer URL from your CIS tenant's OIDC settings
CIS_ISSUER_URL="https://afsp7XXXX.trial-accounts.ondemand.com"

# Paste the Client ID from your created application in CIS
CLIENT_ID="your-client-id-from-cis"

# Paste the Client Secret from your created application in CIS
CLIENT_SECRET="your-client-secret-from-cis"

# The port our local server will run on
PORT=3000

Step 3: The Complete, Working Code (app.js)

This is the full code. The key fix for the openid.Issuer is not a constructor error is using await Issuer.discover() instead of the old constructor method.

require('dotenv').config();
const express = require('express');
const https = require('https');
const fs = require('fs');
const { Issuer } = require('openid-client');

const app = express();
const port = process.env.PORT || 3000;

// Environment variables check
console.log('Checking environment variables:');
console.log('CIS_ISSUER_URL:', process.env.CIS_ISSUER_URL);
console.log('CLIENT_ID:', process.env.CLIENT_ID);
console.log('CLIENT_SECRET:', process.env.CLIENT_SECRET ? '*** (configured)' : 'not set');
console.log('PORT:', process.env.PORT);

// HTTPS options
const httpsOptions = {
  key: fs.readFileSync('key.pem'),
  cert: fs.readFileSync('cert.pem')
};

// Custom Issuer configuration for trial account
async function getCustomIssuer() {
  try {
    console.log('Fetching Issuer configuration...');
    
    const issuer = await Issuer.discover(process.env.CIS_ISSUER_URL);
    console.log('Issuer configuration successful:', issuer.issuer);
    
    return issuer;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Issuer configuration error:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}

// OIDC client initialization
async function initOIDCClient() {
  try {
    const issuer = await getCustomIssuer();
    
    const client = new issuer.Client({
      client_id: process.env.CLIENT_ID,
      client_secret: process.env.CLIENT_SECRET,
      redirect_uris: [`https://localhost:${port}/callback`],
      response_types: ['code'],
      token_endpoint_auth_method: 'client_secret_basic'
    });
    
    console.log('OIDC client initialized successfully');
    return client;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Client initialization error:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}

// Authentication flow
app.get('/login', async (req, res) => {
  try {
    console.log('Starting login process...');
    const client = await initOIDCClient();
    const authUrl = client.authorizationUrl({
      scope: 'openid profile email',
      redirect_uri: `https://localhost:${port}/callback`
    });
    
    console.log('Generated authentication URL:', authUrl);
    res.redirect(authUrl);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Login error:', error);
    res.status(500).send(`Initialization error: ${error.message}`);
  }
});

// Callback processing
app.get('/callback', async (req, res) => {
  try {
    console.log('Callback received');
    const client = await initOIDCClient();
    const params = client.callbackParams(req);
    
    console.log('Exchanging token...');
    const tokenSet = await client.callback(
      `https://localhost:${port}/callback`,
      params
    );
    
    console.log('Fetching user information...');
    const userInfo = await client.userinfo(tokenSet);
    console.log('User information retrieved:', userInfo.sub);
    
    res.send(`
      <!DOCTYPE html>
      <html lang="en">
      <head>
        <meta charset="UTF-8">
        <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
        <title>Authentication Successful</title>
        <style>
          body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; line-height: 1.6; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 20px; }
          h1 { color: #2e7d32; }
          pre { background: #f5f5f5; padding: 15px; border-radius: 4px; overflow-x: auto; }
          .user-info { background: #e8f5e9; padding: 15px; border-radius: 4px; margin-bottom: 20px; }
          .btn { display: inline-block; padding: 10px 20px; background: #4CAF50; color: white; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 4px; }
        </style>
      </head>
      <body>
        <h1>Authentication Successful!</h1>
        
        <div class="user-info">
          <p><strong>Username:</strong> ${userInfo.name || userInfo.email}</p>
          <p><strong>User ID:</strong> ${userInfo.sub}</p>
        </div>
        
        <h2>User Information:</h2>
        <pre>${JSON.stringify(userInfo, null, 2)}</pre>
        
        <hr>
        <a class="btn" href="/">Back to Home</a>
      </body>
      </html>
    `);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Callback processing error:', error);
    res.status(500).send(`
      <h1>Authentication Error</h1>
      <p>${error.message}</p>
      <a href="/">Back to Home</a>
    `);
  }
});

// Home page
app.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.send(`
    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charset="UTF-8">
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
      <title>SAP CIS Authentication Demo</title>
      <style>
        body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; line-height: 1.6; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 40px 20px; text-align: center; }
        h1 { color: #1565c0; }
        .container { background: #e3f2fd; padding: 30px; border-radius: 8px; }
        .btn { display: inline-block; padding: 12px 24px; background: #1565c0; color: white; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 18px; margin-top: 20px; }
        .note { background: #fff8e1; padding: 15px; border-radius: 4px; margin-top: 30px; text-align: left; }
      </style>
    </head>
    <body>
      <div class="container">
        <h1>SAP CIS Authentication Demo</h1>
        <p>This demo shows how to authenticate with SAP Cloud Identity Services</p>
        <a class="btn" href="/login">Login with SAP CIS</a>
      </div>
      
      <div class="note">
        <h3>Browser Security Note:</h3>
        <p>If you see a security warning in your browser:</p>
        <ul>
          <li>Chrome: Type "thisisunsafe" in the address bar</li>
          <li>Others: Go to "Advanced Settings" → "Proceed to localhost"</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </body>
    </html>
  `);
});

// Development: Skip SSL certificate validation
process.env.NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED = '0';

// Start HTTPS server
https.createServer(httpsOptions, app).listen(port, () => {
  console.log('='.repeat(60));
  console.log(`App running: https://localhost:${port}`);
  console.log(`Login URL: https://localhost:${port}/login`);
  console.log('='.repeat(60));
  
  console.log('\n⚠️ Browser Access Instructions:');
  console.log('1. Go to "https://localhost:3000"');
  console.log('2. For security warnings:');
  console.log('   - Chrome: Type "thisisunsafe" in the address bar');
  console.log('   - Others: Click "Advanced" → "Proceed to localhost"');
});

Step 4: Run the Application

You are now ready to test the entire flow.

  1. Run the server from your terminal:
    node app.js
  2. Open your browser and navigate to https://localhost:3000.
  3. Your browser will show a security warning because we are using a self-signed certificate. This is expected. Click "Advanced" and then "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)".
  4. Click the "Login with SAP CIS" link.
  5. You will be redirected to the SAP CIS login page. Enter your credentials.
  6. After successful login, you will be redirected back to your local application, which will display your user information.

Conclusion

This experiment successfully proves that SAP Cloud Identity Services is a fully compliant OIDC provider, capable of securing not just BTP-native applications but any external application that speaks open standards. While the Approuter/XSUAA pattern remains the gold standard for integrated BTP solutions, it's empowering to have this flexibility for prototyping and external integrations.

I hope this detailed guide helps you connect your own applications

entry.title: Re: Credit Note Adjustment through API in Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Mithundogra ,

You can use public API - API_CustomerClearing_SRV in a custom app, which allows automatic clearing of customer line items. Trigger clearing of customer open lines programmatically. You can use assignment feild to group the open items based on your business logic.
Once items are grouped, schedule background jobs using a job scheduler to automate the process.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: How to prepare your SAP S/4HANA Conversion Project.
entry.content:
Hi David, @David when will ALM include a Brownfields path / methodology / steps / accelerators please?

All the QGates emphasis is on "Clean Core" and "Greenfields" questionnaires...which are not relevant to most customers only performing the technical upgrade to Public or Private clouds...

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud: Depreciation Key for Half Year Conventi...
entry.content:
Hi @Elen ,

You cannot chnage period control method in public cloud. The only way to address this is raise Expert configuration request to SAP via CBC tool. Generally, SAP will not assist unless its a regulatory change. I have never seen under IFRS chart anyone using Acq=04. You might also need new depreciation key if SAP assign new PCM to IFRS chart of depreciation. 

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: We would like to print Form 1099 as a PDF with the layout and boxes in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community

We would like to print Form 1099 as a PDF with the layout and boxes in SAP Public Cloud, as shown in the image. The pre-printed file of Run Statutory Report does not include the form boxes. How should we configure the system to generate the complete form?

 

SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Auto-populate Supplier Field in Asset Master When Ass...
entry.content:
Hi Divyesh,

Thank you very much for your clear explanation and helpful suggestion.

Just to clarify: as this is a customization, it should be handled by a developer or technical consultant using ABAP code (e.g., via BAdI implementation or custom logic), right?

Appreciate your support again.

Best regards,

Thanh Ha Le

entry.title: Re: Overwrite dimension filters in SAC Add-in with date r...
entry.content: Hi Nikhil , Currently I facing issue override dimensions members based on filter value . I have product dimension and has plant is attribute . User will select plant as selection , based on this plant ,products need to display in row axis same BPC EPM dimension override . I am using below synatx , not giving #VALUE error . =SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER("Table1","D_Product","Plant=&F11",0,0) ..cells F11 has plant value came from plant selection using SAP.GETDIMENSIONFILTER funcation ....Please advise any inputs . Thanks,

entry.title: Re: OpenText VIM 7.6 SP3 for SAP ECC
entry.content: Hi Divyesh,

entry.title: Re: BCS POP UP while saving a contract agreement - ME31K
entry.content:
Hi @walter_mattos ,

If the system can derive all required BCS fields automatically, the popup will not appear.
Within manage your solution app, search SSCUI 102639 – Define derivation strategy for account assignment in funds management.
Enhance your derivation logic, use your source feilds such as cost centre or G/L Account as source fields and tie them with a constant logic to target feilds in pop-up to Fund centre, commitment item number etc.
So when next time pop-up comes it will fill a constant vlaue and process further.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: OpenText VIM 7.6 SP3 for SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi Divyesh,
1. Check Logs
Reply: History and Workflow log is showing the VIM Document is indeed Posted and Workflow Completed

2. Check if DP Document was Created
Reply: Created and Posted

3. Check Background Jobs Ensure these jobs are running:
/n/spro > VIM Customizing > Schedule Background Jobs
/PTGWFI/WORKFLOW_EXECUTE
/PTGWFI/FETCH_DOCS_FROM_ICC
/PTGWFI/START_WORKFLOW
Reply: Program above does not exist

4. Validate Vendor/PO Mapping If documents are not matched to vendor/PO, they may remain in "Indexed" 
Reply: Document is actually posted correctly, just the VIM analytics (New VAN) status are remain Indexed, but if we are checking from /n/opt/vim_analytics it show correct status of Posted

5. Restart Workflow (If Safe) 
Reply: Workflow has completed and posted

entry.title: Re: BAPI Commit in RAP
entry.content: @SanketGode45 Which environment are you using? S/4HANA on-premise/private cloud edition, S/4HANA public cloud edition or BTP ABAP environment?

entry.title: Re: Auto-populate Supplier Field in Asset Master When Ass...
entry.content: Yes, this must be handled by an ABAP developer using the custom logic app and badi.

entry.title: Re: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard and Universal Model OData Integration with S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Paul,

thank you for the insightful blog. Have a question regarding pushing of data from PaPM cloud to S/4HANA.

in an example of a tax use case, how do we push the data from PaPM cloud to S/4HANA? will it be via the remote function adapter? 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_PROFITABILITY_PERFORMANCE_MANAGEMENT_CLOUD/56471df1959f4cfd9e3bf7a6d2d5be42/a95f96cc2b4d4d268ebc4494d933dd09.html?q=remote+function+adapter

thank you!

Regards,

XQ

entry.title: 过账货物移动(migo)app的快捷操作方式咨询
entry.content: 各位专家老师，想问一下关于过账货物移动(migo)app中，做201或202的发货操作时，有没有快捷的方式，比如批量导入，或全量复制数据等方式，实现更加快速的操作，谢谢！

entry.title: In the context of Data Services an unknown internal server error occurred
entry.content: App :Reprocessing the bank statement items, after inputting Account type :Customer code the error message appears with "In the context of Data Services an unknown internal server error occurred"

entry.title: Re: Custom WDV Depreciation Key for Depreciation Area 15...
entry.content:
Hi @SMK_Ganesh ,

 

Check 2 things in your configuration.

1. Under Period control method for deprication area 15, ensure you use Acquisition=07 which is WDV half-year rule.

2. In your depri key (INWC) cut-off percentage= 50% and use method WDV. If you're unable to edit standard depriciation key then request one via expert configuration request to sap.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: In the context of Data Services an unknown internal s...
entry.content:
Hi @XXuan ,

Verify 2 things.

1. Check your user authorization, if yuo have proper access to process bank statements.

2. Verify your customer code entered valid and correctly assigned to company code.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: In the context of Data Services an unknown internal s...
entry.content: Hi Divyesh,

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA On-premises and S/4HANA private cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Adventurer ,

You won't find T-codes referenced anymore in public cloud scope items, because public cloud is 100% Fiori-based and does not support GUI/tables directly.
If you want to show demo to your client then use S/4HANA private cloud (RISE) or any On-Prem system like IDES.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: In the context of Data Services an unknown internal s...
entry.content:
Hi Divyesh,

Thank you for advice.

There is no user authorization changes to the user.

And as checked the customer code entered is valid and correct also.

And after closing the error message window, I continued the clean process as normal.

entry.title: SAP SAC excel table formate based on propery values
entry.content:
Hi ,

Based latest realse on SAC excel addin futures , I tried cell styles and table formate options and unable to get desired results . please share some inputs on below 

1. How to formate based on dimension propery valsues 

2. How to formate based only base memebrs dyanmically 

Thanks,

Chanti .

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020 and 2025 - 32-bit Connections?
entry.content:
You are correct, both are now 64 bit only and won't detect 32 bit DSN's.

No reason to use 32 bit anymore, all mother boards support 643 bit OS's and it's much faster than 32 bit.

I suggest contacting the ODBC Makers and asking them for a 64 bit version.

entry.title: Re: Custom logic for checking journal entry submitter aga...
entry.content:
Dear Divyesh,

thanks for your support, I checked the Badi BADI_FCLM_APAR_SZENARIO, wasn't available on Public Cloud version, please help to check again.

Patrick

entry.title: Re: In the context of Data Services an unknown internal s...
entry.content: This might be due to cache clearing issue. Try logging out, clearing cache, or use an incognito/private browser window. Run the process again from scratch.

entry.title: Re: Integrating with the Alerts System API in SAP Service Cloud V2

entry.title: Re: 2820775 – Source of Information for Field in structur...
entry.content: Please send your email. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Product Varian for Purchasing
entry.content: Hi Enda, I will explore first then come back to you. Thank you!!!

entry.title: Re: BAPI Commit in RAP
entry.content: @MioYasutake , I am using on-premise environment.

entry.title: Re: Become Part of the SAP TechEd Family as a Community Talk Speaker!
entry.content: The only way I can get into SAP conferences these days as a speaker as via talking about AI. That is quite sad really. AI has it is not a silver bullet, despite what many people say. Let me put it another way - leaving AI aside which programming language is evolving fastest - is it ABAP, Java or JavaScript?

entry.title: Re: Custom logic for checking journal entry submitter aga...
entry.content: You are right. Thats for on prem version. Use Extensibility app and explore relevant released BAdIs. One i recall is BADI_FCLM_CF_ITEM_MODIFY – sometimes available in newer releases of Public Cloud (check your version).

entry.title: Re: File System Full false Alert : Filesystem=/var/opt/osit/tmp/supportconfig
entry.content: How did you treat it as false alert? Could you please provide more details?

entry.title: Re: Unable to find deposit payment processing capability for Lease Out Scope Item 21Q
entry.content:
Hi when you refer to a deposit what do you mean with this exactly? Is this a bond that is received prior to the lease commencement which is either partially or fully returned at the end of the lease?

Or are you referring to the first 2 months rent must be paid in advance prior to commencement of the lease contract?

In other words is the deposit considered a rent payment?

Regards, Andre

entry.title: Open Past Period
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have an issue. I already open for period 06 and 07 2025.

Then I want to posting for period 05 2025. How I do that?

Thank you!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Regarding CAP Java target and custom error messages
entry.content: Hi @davidlam @Ivan-Mirisola , I am looking for the same issue ..As of today do we have any annotation for error message to be added to the field in entity label..So that the custom error message send from backend directly mapped to UI fields..

entry.title: Fiori - supplier Evaluation - No data available
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am using standard Fiori app F1663A, F1661A, F1664A and F2019. If we execute the app always, we receive no data available.

Odata services were also activated but data was not extracted. kindly help to resolve this issues.

entry.title: Re: Missing selection box column on UI Table ABAP RAP
entry.content:
I think if you are looking to enable multi select option in your List report. You have to first adapt and create UI out of it using BAS. In your BAS after you create project using Fiori element List report template , go to your manifest.json and add below property.

{
"pages": {
"ListReport": {
"entitySet": "YourEntitySet",
"multi-select": true
}
}
}

entry.title: Re: Supplier Evaluation in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I am using app id F1663A,F1661A,F1664A and F2019 . We execute the program, always receive no data available.

kindly help me to resolve this issues.

entry.title: Write off approval by standard WS21000102 FICA_WRTOFF and event 5512
entry.content:
The write-off is carried out automatically by saving FP04 using the configured write-off reason and the G/L account - at the same time, the work item for the approval workflow is generated. If approval is not granted or denied in individual cases, the derecognition is automatically cancelled and reversed.

How to manage workflow in this case?

entry.title: Re: Having Trouble Uploading XSD File to "Define Statutor...
entry.content:
Hi HuyNguyen1,

Understanding the Error

The error message ‘File YY1_XXX.xsd does not have any entry points. Statutory Reporting - Creating a Schema Definition Upload a valid file.’ indicates that the XSD file you are trying to upload lacks a defined entry point. An entry point in an XSD file is typically the root element that the schema defines, which is necessary for the system to understand the starting point for processing the file.

Possible Causes

  1. Missing Root Element: The XSD file might not have a root element defined, which is essential for the schema to be valid.
  2. Incorrect File Structure: The file might not be structured correctly according to the expected schema format.

How to Resolve

  1. Check the XSD File: Ensure that the XSD file has a root element defined. You can open the file in a text editor and look for the <xs:element name="root"> or similar structure.
  2. Validate the XSD: Use an XML editor or online tool to validate the XSD file to ensure it meets the required standards.

Additional Tips

  • Consult Documentation: Refer to SAP documentation on creating XSD files for statutory reporting to ensure compliance with the expected format.
  • Use Sample XSDs: You can refer to sample XSDs provided in SAP documentation to understand the correct structure.

If the issue persists, consider reaching out to SAP support for further assistance.

Statutory Reporting - Creating a Schema Definition

entry.title: Re: Stock Transfer for Reservation
entry.content:
Hello Chuma,

When we do Stock Transfer for Reservation, the batches are mandatory in the App 'Stock Transfer for Reservation'.

Batches should be consider only while creating the Warehouse Task in 'Run Outbound Process - Deliveries' App.

Could you please help ?

Thanks & Regards,

Mithila

entry.title: Re: Error: AI API call failed on the tutorial
entry.content:
Hello @DarkoB,

I'm facing the same issue in both tutorials:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-helloworld.html

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-code.html

Error Code: ERR_BAD_REQUEST
Message: Request failed with status code 403

Did you find out any solution?

Thx

Daniel

entry.title: 【sap public cloud】BUSINESS_PARTNER POST ERROR
entry.content:
Nice to meet you.

I am trying to add supplier master data to "BusinessPartner" in "SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud" using tRestClient in "Talend8 Cloud".

The JSON format data to be passed to tRestClient can be registered successfully in POSTMAN, but the following error occurs in Talend. It would be appreciated if you could tell me what the cause is.

Error while parsing an XML stream: 'BOM / charset detection failed'.

 

＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝

はじめまして

「Talend8 cloud」で「sap s/4hana public cloud」の「API_BUSINESS_PARTNER」へtRestclientを使い、サプライヤマスタの追加を試しています。

tRestclientへ渡すJSON形式のデータは、POSTMANでは正常に登録可能なのですが、Talendだと以下のエラーが発生しております。何が原因かご教示頂けると助かります。

Error while parsing an XML stream: 'BOM / charset detection failed'.

entry.title: Re: 🌟 Community Spotlight: Our Top Contributors June 2025 🌟
entry.content: Congratulations!!!

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 31: Cloud Identity Services - Identity Directory Service
entry.content:
In this video, we explain how cloud identity services handle authorizations using the identity directory service. The process involves choosing between using a user store or not, with the latter relying on corporate identity providers for group mapping. By enabling the user store, the system uses Cloud Identity's authentication service and enriches claims with additional properties such as user groups. In the video we discuss two ways to manage authorizations: through AD/LDAP/EntraID/... groups or Cloud Identity service user groups, offering flexibility for organizations in handling user access.

Takeaways

  • Cloud Identity Service can act as a proxy or use a user store for authorizations.
  • Without a user store, authorization relies on AD/LDAP/EntraID/... groups from a corporate identity provider.
  • Enabling the user store allows for the use of Cloud Identity Service for both authentication and authorization.
  • When the user store is enabled, user groups from Cloud Identity Service are used to map BTP Role Collections.
  • Corporate identity provider is used purely for authentication when the user store is enabled.
  • Using the identity directory service with a user store enriches claims from the corporate identity provider.
  • User store enables more direct control over user authorizations without relying on external groups.
  • Two options for managing authorizations in BTP: AD/LDAP/EntraID/... groups or Cloud Identity Service user groups.
  • Enabling the user store ensures more granular and integrated control over user identity and roles.

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Keeping up to date with tariff changes
entry.content:
In our new global trade reality, keeping track of tariff changes can seem an insurmountable task. Trade policies are changing more rapidly than ever before and being enforced at a moment’s notice; requiring flexibility and adaptability from companies to implement new tariffs to ensure that they are not penalised for underpaying duties.

If continuously monitoring customs authorities’ official communication channels is the most straight forward option, it comes with great resource constraint. And a certain dose of expertise to determine which change applies to the organization, in what context, etc.

Another option is engaging with a licensed customs broker. These experts would be able to provide the level of monitoring and guidance mentioned above, on behalf of the organization.

But there is a third option for companies wishing to do it themselves and leverage these uncertain times to gain a competitive advantage by reacting faster and more precisely: aligning best-in-class content with best-in-class technology from SAP Global Trade Services to get information in real time and directly connected to the core processes and supply chain.

 

How can companies get the latest duty rates?

 

As per leading analyst firm Gartner, there are two types of global trade management software providers: those that do and those that do not provide content with their tools.

On this matter, SAP has strategically decided to work with its ecosystem of professional global trade data partners offering subscription-based access to a wide selection of trade content. Content from dedicated teams that specialize in identifying, interpreting, and collecting changes and updates to trade regulations that can be imported into SAP through standardized interfaces, but, more importantly, content that can be tailored to each customer’s specific needs in terms of geographic coverage (regions and countries to monitor), content types, and process areas.

This provides organizations with the freedom to select a global trade data partner covering their specific requirements or even combine content from various providers.

As a result, the global trade data partner acts as a central source for up-to-date information helping companies navigate evolving trade regulations. Information provided includes, but not limited to, customs tariff measures and duty rates, export control lists, rules for preferential origin determination for free trade agreements, and more.

Companies such as Mendel Verlag started decades ago with production and publication of customs tariffs and other publications and have expanded to electronic formats that can be directly consumed by global trade management platforms such as SAP Global Trade Services.

To produce this foreign trade content, Mendel Verlag follows a 5-step approach – all done internally by a team of specialists:

  1. Research for the identification and review of relevant sources
  2. Compile information from step 1
  3. Evaluate for relevance and validity
  4. Consolidate different information and sources for integrated data
  5. Quality control including classic editing but also manual checks of electronically processed data

This last step is crucial, as only with expert review can the content provider ensure credibility and reliability of the trade compliance master data information. As highlighted by Mendel Verlag: “Quality is a key requirement of our work. Only valid data can be used effectively in foreign trade and customs processes.”

Once subscribed to a data content service, customers will receive continuous updates.

To ensure that the trade compliance information in SAP Global Trade Services is in sync and therefore also continuously up to date, the procedure is quite simple. The updated file from the content provider – usually in an XML format, is placed on a secure file transfer protocol (SFTP) folder where it is picked up by SAP Global Trade Services and values are uploaded into the platform.  This process includes control parameter checks, and errors can be manually reviewed.

If a company decides to change data providers, and therefore need to replace the previous data provider's content with the new data provider's content, SAP Global Trade Services can of course enable the configuration of time-dependency of this content. This flexibility enables customers to enter two competing sets of content in the system, and when they activate the new data provider's content, the old data provider's content – and all dependent items, is deactivated automatically.

 

Typical misconceptions

 

We, at SAP, strongly believe that working hand-in-hand with a global trade data partner provider is in the best interest of our customers to be able to freely choose, change or combine content from various content providers, addressing specific countries or regions, content types and process areas.

This can raise a few questions and misconceptions:

 

Misconception

Reality

It is more expensive to have the content and the software from different providers

The cost of the content itself is much less than the cost of running a global trade management system, and you pay for it, even when bundled in “one software package”

It is more hassle to manage

Once the one-time setup is completed, updates will flow via certified and tested interfaces with little to no manual intervention (only in case of errors or exceptions)

This approach constraints customers

Quite the opposite, it offers the freedom to pick and choose the best provider for the requirement (compliance area, countries where the company has trade operations, industry, etc.)

SAP recommends this because it is not a content provider

This is absolutely true! In the area of trade compliance, SAP is not a content provider. SAP strategically chose to work with external, specialized, third-party vendors. SAP focuses on delivering the necessary system logic, the workflow, and the integration with core systems and business processes

As eloquently stated by Christian Stadler – Chief Product Owner for SAP International Trade Management portfolio: “working with content partners such as Mendel Verlag makes our SAP solutions even more relevant, and constantly adapted to rapidly changing situations”

If you are interested in discovering more about SAP Global Trade Services, see our dedicated website and associated resources: videos, customer stories, and more.

I would like to thank Mendel Verlag for their collaboration on this article.

Originally published on June 30, 2025 & reposted on this platform

entry.title: Re: Primeira edição do SAP XPERIENCE CWB: um sábado para ficar na história!
entry.content: Parabéns pelo evento!

entry.title: Re: Validating the Valuation Class in Material Master MM01
entry.content:
1. Create a new Account Category Reference (ACR) in OMSK
  2. Assign this newly created ACR to Valuation Class in OMSK
  3. Also, assign this ACR to material type in OMSK

By doing so, users can only use the available Valuation Class

entry.title: Re: Hosting Experience - Fireside Chat with AWS Leaders
entry.content: Amazing!

entry.title: Re: NA
entry.content: Hello, what can we help here?

entry.title: Re: Sap Analytics Cloud - Security for an end user to be...
entry.content:
@BeatrizDuenas If the rejection is due to DAC. I think you need to examine the DAC. Couple of things you could do to better isolate the issue.

  • Try importing the data using a normal import job. Examine the rejected records.
  • Try importing the data without DAC on model
  • Try importing by by adjusting the DAC. Also as DAC is based on dimension member. Try loading data for only the member you have write access given.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Please tell me how to check which users IT Contact is set for.
entry.content:
Please tell me how to check which users IT Contact is set for.

Is there a way to check this from SAP for Me?
What other ways are there to check this?

I want to know which user is currently set as the IT Contact before changing it.

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Order app does not show custom docume...
entry.content: I have tried this already. If the document was created with NBF, it shows in the app. But wherever the field selection was changed, these custom document types do not show in the Manage Purchase order app.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Creating a Fiori tile for ABAP reports
entry.content:
TL;DR – How to create a tile on the SAP Fiori launchpad to run an ABAP report – based on customer experiences. This is a gap-filling technique that is useful for moving existing custom ABAP reports in system conversion projects, or where an SAP predefined report is missing from the SAP Fiori apps reference library.   Alternatives are given at the end of the blog post..

You have an existing ABAP report that you want to run from the SAP Fiori launchpad.  How do you create a tile to run an ABAP report? In the example in this blog post you will see how you can create a tile using the predefined transaction SA38. This is a simple 3-step approach for a sandbox environment. For a development environment, you should consider creating a custom transaction. You will find a brief discussion at the end of this blog post in section Alternative approaches to consider.

 

Creating a tile from scratch is a 3-step process:

  1. Define the tile to launch the ABAP report.
  2. Reference the tile definition into your custom business role for the user.
  3. Adjust the role’s menu and authorizations for any authorizations needed to run the ABAP report.

Once you test your tile you can consider optional Next Steps.

Why create a tile for an ABAP report?

When transitioning from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, you're shifting from using SAP GUI to the SAP Fiori launchpad. This change lets you take advantage of new features offered by RISE with SAP S/4HANA via SAP Fiori apps. You've identified these new functionalities using tools like SAP Signavio. Refer to SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Finding business process improvements with SAP Signavio

Due to time and budget constraints, it's often not feasible to convert all existing user interfaces into SAP Fiori apps. You want to avoid creating a mixed experience for users; instead, you want them to access all applications—both new and old—from a single platform.

The SAP Fiori launchpad enables you to integrate classic UIs, including GUI Transactions, Web Dynpro ABAP applications, WebClient UIs, and ABAP Reports. Many SAP GUI transactions and ABAP Reports are already available in the SAP Fiori apps reference library by filtering on All Apps for SAP S/4HANA.

However, custom ABAP "Z" reports and some SAP predefined ABAP reports, like batch-scheduled ones, may not be listed. For these situations, you might need to manually create a tile in the SAP Fiori launchpad.

Hints:

  • Refer to SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Administration Guide for how to create a tile for any GUI transaction. 
  • If you are not familiar with the terms technical catalog, business catalog, business role, or Launchpad App Descriptor Item (LADI), you can find these explained in the SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad – Administration Guide – Setting up Launchpad Content – About Launchpad Content
  • All screenshots shown on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 system.  This same process can be used on SAP S/4HANA 2020 or higher.

Example Use Case

For customers using Project and Portfolio Management, it can be helpful to extend project roles such as Project Financial Controller (technical id SAP_BR_PROJ_FIN_CONTROLLER) with some additional ABAP reports that are not yet included in the SAP Fiori apps reference library.  

For example, report /RPM/FICO_INT_PLANNING_V2 is an ABAP report that can be scheduled, or optionally run manually. To run the report manually you want a tile on the SAP Fiori launchpad. You use this report to control when and how data from SAP Financials and Controlling (FI-CO) is a applied to projects managed by Portfolio and Project Management. 

Prerequisites

Assumptions:

  • Your SAP Fiori launchpad for SAP S/4HANA is already configured and active. If this has not been done, refer to Learning Video - Activating SAP Fiori Launchpad Using SAP Fiori Foundation Task List

You will need to know:

  • The ABAP report id.  This could be a SAP predefined ABAP report or a custom ABAP report.
  • The technical catalog that will hold the tile definition.
  • The business catalog that will hold the reference to the tile definition.
  • Which business role contains the business catalog.
  • A test user id assigned to the business role that will contain the tile.
  • 1 cross-client workbench transport request
  • 1 client-specific customizing transport request
  • Package Id for your launchpad content.

Pro-tip: It's helpful to keep all SAP Fiori launchpad content in the same package.

For the example use case:

  • Custom technical catalog Z_TC_PROJ_FIN_EXTRA has been created to hold custom apps for Project Financials.
  • Custom business catalog Z_BC_PROJ_FIN_PORTFOLIO has been created to collect custom apps for Project Finance – Portfolio Management
  • The business catalog Z_BC_PROJ_FIN_PORTFOLIO has already been assigned to custom business role Z_BR_PROJ_FIN_CONTROLLER.

You will need access to Fiori launchpad content administration tools including:

  • Launchpad App Manager
  • Launchpad Content Manager Client-Specific
  • Role Maintenance (transaction PFCG)
  • Report execution transaction SA38

Hint: For this example, the Fiori Administration role (id Z_FIORI_FOUNDATION_ADMIN) has been used. This business role is generated by task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4. Refer to Learning Video - Activating SAP Fiori Launchpad Using SAP Fiori Foundation Task List generated by task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4. Authorizations to execute transactions PFCG and PFCGUPDATEROLEMENU were added to the admin role. In practice, the role maintenance tasks would be run by a security administrator user.

Step 1 – Define the tile for the ABAP Report

First you need to define the tile for the SAP Fiori launchpad.

  • You define the tile by creating Launchpad App Descriptor Item (LADI). A LADI contains the tile definition that defines how the tile should appear, and the target mapping that defines which app is launched when the tile is selected in the launchpad.  
  • You define the tile once, and then later you reference the tile to one or more business roles. You typically define the tile in your Sandbox or Development system, then transport the changes to subsequent systems in your landscape as needed.
  • LADIs are created within cross-client technical catalogs. Because technical catalogs are cross-client, you will use a Workbench Transport Request to capture your changes ready for transport. Refer to SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad – Administration Guide – Setting up launchpad content - Setting Up Technical Catalogs with the SAP Fiori Launchpad App Manager

Login as your Fiori launchpad content administrator.

Open the Launchpad App Manager.

On the tab Technical Catalogs, find and open the technical catalog that will contain the LADI definition.

Hint: You can optionally create a new standard catalog using the button New Standard Catalog on the same tab.

Select the option Add App > Transaction to add a new LADI for a transaction.

You will be asked to confirm the Package and Workbench Transport Request to capture your changes to the technical catalog.

For the field Transaction Code, enter SAP GUI transaction SA38.

Pro-tip: You should use transaction SA38 for business users executing reports. To avoid unnecessary authorizations being generated, do NOT use transaction SE38 which should only be used by developers who create reports.

Press the <ENTER> key. This will default in some information from SE93 such as:

  • Device Type settings
  • Application Component id
  • Default tile name

Adjust the following settings in tab Target Application Fields:

  • Semantic Object – use the closest SAP Semantic object that fits with the main business object of the report.  For the example, because the report is run for project portfolios, the standard semantic object ProjectPortfolio is selected.
  • Action – The action for the Semantic Object is freely definable. Enter a camel case verb that fits with purpose of the report. Action is set to integrateFICO4Planning
  • Target Application Title – this is the link text that is shown whenever the app is displayed as a link, e.g. in Search Results, Related Apps, and List of Links dialogs. For the example use case, Target Application Title is set to Portfolio - Financial Periodic Planning and Integration.
  • Application Component ID – this is the support component shown in the About and App Support dialogs to indicate where to search on SAP for Me for information. For the example, Application Component ID is set to PPM-PFM.

Pro-tip: There are more than 3K Semantic Objects delivered by SAP, with predefined dynamic navigation links to related launchpad contact, including any additional custom tiles you create. Avoid creating custom Semantic Objects as these are not linked to any existing apps, making it harder for users to navigate between apps.

Add the following parameters in tab Parameters:

  • Program – set Default Value to your program id, and set Rename To value  RS38M-PROGRAMM
  • DynproNoFirstScreen  - set Default Value = 1
  • DYNP-OKCODE  - set Default Value = STRT to start the report

To complete the definition, you adjust the tile appearance in the tab Tiles.

Adjust the following settings in tab Tiles:

  • Tile texts - Title, Subtitle, and Information
  • Tile Keywords – these are used to help find the tile when using Search on Apps
  • Tile icon – you can use the value help to search for suitable icons

Select Save to save your LADI.

Step 2 – Assign the tile definition to the business role

Launchpad content items – such as apps - are directly assigned to business catalogs, and indirectly assigned to business roles. Business catalogs typically contain a small subset of closely related apps that are assigned together to one (or more) business roles.  Business catalogs and business roles are client-specific.  

Open the Launchpad Content Manager – Client-Specific.

On the tab Catalogs, find and open the business catalog that will reference the LADI.

  • You can use an existing business catalog, or you can select Create to create a new business catalog.
  • If you create a new business catalog you will need to assign the business catalog to the business role. 

Find and add the LADI to the business catalog using the option Add Tiles/Target Mappings > Add Tile/TMs to selected catalog.

Find your LADI add reference it to your business catalog by selecting Add Tile/TM Reference.

Confirm the LADI has been added to your business catalog.

To check which roles use this business catalog, select Show Usage in Roles.

 

Step 3 – Adjust the business role content and authorizations

You now need to adjust the related business role's content and authorizations.  The easiest way to do this is to use transaction PFCGUPDATEROLEMENU. This transaction runs program PRGN_COMPARE_ROLE_MENU.  You can also adjust the role manually in Role Maintenance transaction PFCG.

Run transaction PFCGUPDATEROLEMENU for the related business role(s).

Any mismatches with the current role content are highlighted by the traffic light in the first column.

Select the mismatched row(s). Adjust the role menu by selecting Adapt Menu.

Press Refresh to review the corrected role content.  Correcting the role content impacts the role's authorization status, which needs to be adjusted for the change in content.

Select Change Role to review and adjust the changes in the role's authorizations. 

Find the new entries for Authorization Object S_PROGRAM in class BC_C. These grant authorization to run the program. 

Adjust the authorization program setting the program id. Set authorization field P_PROGRAM to the program you want the user to run.

Adjust the activities so that the user can run the report. Set authorization field P_ACTION to SUBMIT.  Pro-tip: You can also set VARIANT if you want the user to save variants to minimize data entry. 

Generate the role to update the profile.

Confirm the authorizations profile status is now generated.

Pro-tip: If you want to use the changes immediately you may need to run a User Comparison in the Role Maintenance transaction PFCG. This updates the cache that holds the user's current authorizations. 

Review the User tab.  Make sure your test user is assigned to the role. Run the User Comparison for your test user.

Test your tile

You can now use your test user to test your tile from SAP Fiori launchpad using the App Finder.

Select the tile using your business catalog title or any part of the keywords or tile texts.

Select the tile to launch the report.

Next steps – consider where to place your tile

Once you are satisfied the tile is working, you can decide where to place it to help the user find it during their day-to-day work.  For example, you can add the tile to any Page assigned to any of the role’s spaces.

You can find which space is assigned to your business role using either:

  • Launchpad Content Manager – Client Specific - tab Roles - option Show Spaces
  • Overview of Role, Spaces and Pages (transaction /UI2/RSP_LIST)

Refer to SAP Help – SAP Fiori launchpad – Administration Guide – Analyzing Spaces and Pages

Worth knowing: You maintain spaces in app F4834 Manage Launchpad Spaces. You maintain the related pages in app F4512 Manage Launchpad Pages.  Refer to Learning Video - Creating SAP Fiori Launchpad Layouts for Custom Business Roles.

 

Alternative approaches to consider

For improved usage tracking and role maintenance

Instead of using transaction SA38, you can define a custom transaction for your ABAP report id.  Using a custom transaction enables you to track the usage of this app by transaction code.  It can also be useful for the security administrator.  For example, from Role Maintenance (transaction PFCG) they can search for the custom transaction across all security roles.

You can define custom transactions in transaction SE93.  For example, you could define a report transaction or a parameter transaction.

  • Instead of entering the parameters in the LADI, you enter the parameters in the custom transaction.
  • Mark your transaction as suitable for SAP GUI for HTML so that others know you have already checked all its features work from the SAP Fiori launchpad. 
  • You then use your custom transaction to create your LADI and complete the changes to the business catalog and business role as explained above.

Refer to: SAP Help - ABAP Platform - ABAP Workbench Tools - Maintaining Transactions

For improved report design and behaviour

Consider updating your ABAP reports to newer versions that are optimized for cloud use and mobile devices. If a report is hard to use, lacks information, or could be improved with visuals, consider redesigning it for a better experience.

For example:

You might consider replacing it with a SAP Fiori elements app,  for example, using the floorplans List Report or Analytical List Page. Refer to SAP Design Guidelines.

  • Pro-tip: Make sure your developers are up-to-date with the latest recommended programming models such as Fiori Elements Flexible Programming Model Explorer and ABAP RESTful Programming Model.

Or you might consider replacing it with a SAP Analytics Cloud story, if you want to combine information from SAP S/4HANA and another solution. Refer to Learning Journey - Designing Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow for Sales for Public Cloud - Complete Guide
entry.content: Hi @Elkhawanky , I have created CMR and  work item created for approval and approver put to rework, Initiator sets it to rework not required. We receive couple of My Inbox notifications created. Please explain what we missed here, we need to receive only one notification in my inbox of the approver.

entry.title: Goods Return Item Serial Number
entry.content:
Hi All

Is it not possible show Item Serial Number in AP Invoice and Goods Return PLD?

I managed to add AP Invoice Rows Serial Number but it is not showing at all in Print Layout Design.

 

 

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Re: Not able to create a new account of BTP trial
entry.content: Yes, I got the same issue. Hopefully, they resolve it soon.

entry.title: Interview Scheduling with Work Tech and Exchange onPremise
entry.content:
Dear all,

is there any way how Interview Scheduling using Work Tech configuration, can work with an Exchange onPremise Setup?
Theoretically it could work, using this Enable Modern Auth in Exchange Server on-premises | Microsoft Learn

All SAP documentation is only regarding M365 and nothing mentioned about other setups.

Best Regards

Sylvester

entry.title: Re: Missing selection box column on UI Table ABAP RAP
entry.content: I created this app not in BAS, basically it's not deployed yet. it is RAP application using eclipse.

entry.title: Re: Unable to find deposit payment processing capability for Lease Out Scope Item 21Q
entry.content:
Hi @andre_nakratzas, thanks for your comment.

We are referring to scenario 1: "a bond that is received prior to the lease commencement which is either partially or fully returned at the end of the lease"

entry.title: Re: EWM-Pick List Printing through Post Processing Framework(PPF)
entry.content: Nice blog

entry.title: Re: problem connecting process automation with build app
entry.content: Are you saying you go to Library and you don't see your process? You must click Library and then select your process.

entry.title: Re: Missing selection box column on UI Table ABAP RAP
entry.content:
Totally depends on your requirement then. Using RAP if you want to enable selection of line item, there must be some action you want to perform after selecting the row. If you just want to navigate to object page(detail), clicking on row item should be ok which lets you go to object page. Object page can be enabled using association in your CDS.

if you have an action to be performed via row select, multi select, you must enable action using annotation @ui.DataFieldForAction under @ui.LineItem. Adding this annotation will enable row selection box in front of each rows.

-BR

Tejas

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Performance analysis tools
entry.content: very helpful

entry.title: Re: SAC Optimized Story Mode SortByValue Table
entry.content:
Hello @maria_ganglmair42,

Coding for table looks a bit different. Following statement should work:

Material.sortByValue({[Alias.MeasureDimension]:selectedMeasure},SortOrder.Ascending,Direction.Vertical);

Best regards

Lothar

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Embedded Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) Overview
entry.content: very useful blog

entry.title: Re: Validating the Valuation Class in Material Master MM01
entry.content:
Hi @javierm_9230 
we can find the linkage between material type - Account category reference - valuation class details in the below configuration path. Or we may check at table T025 (Val. Class and Acc. Cat. Ref.) and T134.
SPRO > MM > Valuation and account assignment > Account determination > without Wizard
while creating/changing material system allows only particular valuation classes configurated in the above path.
Please let us know If you find any Issues.

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC excel table formate based on propery values
entry.content:
@Chanti 

Formatting based on dimension attribute is currently not possible.

For 2. you can use Styling Option on the Designer panel. You can use an existing cell styling or also create new styles from Excel home menu.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Tutorial for Project Management Module in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check the below How to Guide link

Project Management in SAP Business One

This will help you with the fields and working of the Project Management.

Best,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Auto Import to PRE-PRD - in ChaRM
entry.content:
Hi Satish,

Regarding your concern, for auto-import during status change, you could use urgent change transaction, if you want to use normal change, then you need to use the preliminary import scenario in order to use the auto-import function during status change. See below documents for details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/d6e6bcc740964311987d440ab83e6684.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/48db8900a73b04e7e10000000a42189c.html?locale=en-US

Besides these 2 scenarios, there is no standard way to trigger the auto-import during status change(from QA->Pre-PROD). You will then need to involve consulting service to see if customization is feasible or not.

BR

entry.title: Re: Trial balance report not showing BP filter and GL fil...
entry.content: Hi, what is the status with a different user with same finance license?

entry.title: Re: Issue in Cycle count
entry.content:
Hi @LEDL 
My understanding is two physical inventory documents generated for a specific storage location.
Is materials are also same in the PIDs? Hope this issue not occurs.
If one document is counted and the other one is not.. we may check the changes in Mi12 t-code.
Please let us know If find any Issues on the same.

How can I add "SAP Analytics Cloud" ?

Vijay

entry.title: Issue with Posting Supplier Invoice in MIRO – Line Item Limit Exceeded
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We are currently facing an issue while trying to post a supplier invoice using transaction MIRO. The number of line items exceeds the limit of 999, which is preventing us from posting the invoice.

Kindly advise on how to proceed or suggest any possible workarounds.

Thank you for your support.

Vignesh K

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement

Thank you.

Best Regards,

Supatchj.

entry.content: "short dump" means nothing if you don't attach the short dump (full short dump as text file)

entry.title: Re: How to Update DMEE Format CGI_XML_CT to the pain 001....
entry.content: Hello S0026819777 , We used the file CGI_XML_CT_V9_22102024 (version from februar24) and it worked properlly. The new xml version from june.25 in Snote 2253571 should work! please be aware that, if you are using the DMEEX you have to follow the instructions from S-note 2784858.

entry.title: A Step-by-Step Guide to Automatic Account Determination in SAP MM-FI Integration
entry.content:
In SAP, the integration between Materials Management (MM) and Financial Accounting (FI) plays a critical role in ensuring smooth procurement and inventory operations. One of the core components of this integration is Automatic Account Determination - a configuration-driven approach to automatically derive the correct G/L accounts during transactions such as Purchase Orders (PO), Goods Receipt (GR) and Invoice Verification (MIRO).

This blog walks through the step-by-step configuration and use-case setup for both material and non-material-based procurement scenarios.

Step 1: Activate Valuation Grouping Code (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > MM > Valuation and Account Assignment > Account Determination without Wizard > Define Valuation Control

Valuation grouping allows you to group valuation areas (like plants) under a common valuation grouping code, simplifying account determination across plants.

Step 2: Group Valuation Areas (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > MM > Valuation and Account Assignment > Account Determination without Wizard > Group Together Valuation Areas

Assign multiple plants or valuation areas to the same Valuation Grouping Code. This eliminates the need to maintain separate general ledger (G/L) account configurations for each plant.

💡Example: VG Code 0001 groups plants 1000, 1001, and 1234, all under company code 1000.

Step 3: Define Account Category Reference (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > MM > Valuation and Account Assignment > Account Determination without Wizard > Define Valuation Classes

Set up Account Category Reference (ACR) - a 4-digit code linking material types with valuation classes. This controls which valuation class can be assigned to which material type.

We have standard valuation classes based on material type. I have copied them and created valuation classes for each material type, assigning them to the account category reference I had previously created.

There is no direct configuration between material type and valuation class account assignment, elaborating which material types related materials can be chosen for which valuation class. In this step, I have assigned my material type with the account reference category, so that the valuation class will get automatically assigned to the valuation class.

Step 4: Assign Account Category Reference to Material Type

Map the Account Category Reference to the Material Type to ensure automatic derivation of the valuation class during material master creation.

✅This reduces manual input and aligns material types with relevant financial postings.

In the Material Master View

Step 5: Define Account grouping for Movement Types (Transaction: OMWM)

Path: IMG > Material Management > Valuation and account assignment > account determination > account determination without wizard > Define Account Groupings for movement types.

Movement types trigger valuation strings, which link to transaction keys and account modifiers. While SAP provides standard movement types, understanding their link to account determination is essential for troubleshooting and custom processes.

NOTE: This is a step to create a movement type, but we do not make any changes or create a movement type, as SAP has provided all the necessary movement types for the business according to the requirements.

Step 6: Try creating a PO and post a GR against the PO

Create a PO and perform a GR to simulate a real scenario.

This helps validate whether the automatic account determination is working correctly before going live.

From the MM perspective, we know only the process but not the transaction and G/L account in the OBYC settings. To identify this, let’s follow the steps below (Automatic Account Posting) for basic understanding.

Step 7: Automatic Account Posting (Transaction: OBYC)

PATH: IMG > Material Management > Valuation and account assignment > Account determination > Account determination without wizard > Configure automatic postings

Let’s try simulating our scenario for plant 1000, Material HR100000, movement type 101 (GR Goods Receipt)

Use simulation tools to verify how SAP derives the accounts based on:

  • Company Code: 1000
  • Valuation Group Code: 0001
  • Material type: ZROH
  • Valuation class: 1100
  • Movement Type: 101
  • Plant: 1000
  • Chart of Accounts: INT

These posting lines indicate the possible scenarios from the FI perspective for mapping the G/Ls concerning the MM process.

For Ex, Here in our scenario, GR Goods receipt will hit transaction BSX – Inventory Posting and WRX -GR/IR Clearing Account.

Go back and navigate to Account Assignment

Here, assign G/L accounts to relevant transaction keys like:

  • BSX – Inventory Posting

  • WRX – GR/IR Clearing Account

Now, try posting GR for PO with movement type 101

Document posted, check for FI documents

Handling Scenarios Without a Material Master

There are times when you need to procure services or materials without using a material master. This requires a different approach for automatic account determination. 

Step 1: Define material Group (Transaction: OMSF)

Path: SPRO > Logistics – General > Material Master > Settings for Key Fields > Define Material Groups

Material groups help categorise non-material items and are later used to derive G/L accounts.

Step 2: Assign the valuation Class to the Material Group

Path: SPRO > IMG > Material management > purchasing > Material master > entry aids for item without material master

This mapping ensures the system uses a material group-based valuation class during procurement.

Step 3: Assign/Check General Modifier to Account Assignment

Path: SPRO > IMG > Material management > Purchasing > account assignment –maintain account assignment categories

Maintain G/L accounts in OBYC under GBB-VBR (Offsetting Entry for Inventory Posting - Consumption) for relevant valuation classes or material groups.

Step 4: Create Purchase Order (Transaction: ME21N)

Use Account Assignment Category "K" (Cost Centre) and select the relevant material group. The system will automatically pick the G/L account based on OBYC settings.

✅ Transactions triggered:

  • GBB-VBR for inventory offset

  • KBS for cost centre posting

If you found this guide helpful, don’t miss my other SAP MM blogs covering real-world automation, configuration tips, and best practices for procurement and inventory management.

Click to view other SAP MM WM Blogs - SAP Community

 

Regards,

Mannem Leelamohan

entry.title: Re: total shelf life field is not appearing in purchase o...
entry.content:
Hi @suraj9051 
It is the standard design of PO Item delivery tab.
If you want to add 'total shelf life' field, you may need to check with technical team (ABAP) about the possibility.
